channel.title: Toronto Star – Adam Radwanski
channel.description: Articles by Adam Radwanski from the Toronto Star.

item.title: Adam Radwanski: Donald Trump is betting on our weakness. We can’t afford to prove him right
item.description: The next month will be about proving him wrong. And so far, we're not off to a half-bad start, writes Adam Radwanski.

item.title: Adam Radwanski: Under Toronto’s dystopian haze, Canada’s pivot away from climate leadership is impossible to ignore
item.description: The wildfire smoke has choked my faith in Canada's climate future, writes Adam Radwanski.

item.title: Adam Radwanski: Doug Ford’s U.S. booze ban is a winning card in fight against Trump’s stacked trade deck
item.description: More than just about anything else Canada has done in the past 18 months, writes Adam Radwanski, the LCBO U.S. booze ban by Doug Ford has gotten

item.title: Adam Radwanski: Hey, big spender: Why we need to start giving Mark Carney a bit less benefit of the doubt
item.description: Canadians must demand better justification from Mark Carney, and question his "just-trust-me" style of committing billions in public funds without

item.title: Adam Radwanski: Why we should laugh off Doug Ford and Danielle Smith’s costly pipeline scheme
item.description: The Alberta-to-Ontario pipeline proposal lacks economic merit, carries massive taxpayer risks, and should be dismissed as political theatre,

item.title: Adam Radwanski: Why it’s getting harder for Canada to wait for a good deal with Donald Trump
item.description: Canada can no longer afford to play a waiting game for trade relief, with Trump's sectoral tariffs threatening permanent closures in Ontario's

item.title: Adam Radwanski: Carney’s condo controversy is a warning about two of his biggest liabilities
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney's confusing announcement around a condo-buying plan in B.C. continues his government's communication problem, and risks

item.title: Adam Radwanski: The Gordie Howe bridge is still closed and Donald Trump won’t open it. What that says about ‘Fortress North America’
item.description: There are many reasons for Canadians to be furious about Trump blocking the bridge's opening, but there's also a silver lining, writes Adam
